agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity wi

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) electrostatic interactions without compromising SSTR2 binding.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current design strategy is generating high-affinity binders but at the cost

report.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating high-affinity binders but at the cost of reduced selectivity. The focus must shift toward disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to increase Δmargin and achieve the primary objective of SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to focus on mutating positions that are non-pharmacophore but critical for off-target interactions (SSTR3/SSTR5). These positions are known to mediate conserved-pocket contacts.
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 by introducing charged or polar residues that are unlikely to form stable contacts in off-target pockets.
